steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both authors criticize the scope of current modernist scholarship. Text 1 argues scholars overrepresent fragmented narratives and overlook coherent ones like von Arnim’s. Text 2 argues that fixating on “fragmentation versus coherence” inhibits inquiry into other stylistic elements. Both share the view that the field’s current focus is too narrow and produces a limited picture of the period.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Without a major shift in focus, the vision that it presents of fiction written in the period will continue to be unnecessarily limited.": matches both texts. Text 1 shows von Armin stays overlooked when scholars spotlight fragmented narratives. Text 2 states the narrow focus “inhibits inquiry” into other stylistic elements. Both predict or warn that the field remains limited without broader focus. ✓
  • "Instead of engaging in unproductive debates, it should work to rehabilitate the reputations of neglected modernist works.": neither author frames the current state as a debate to be avoided. Both critique the field’s narrow scope, not the quality of its argumentation. ✗
  • "Its primary methods for analyzing fiction written in the period are growing obsolete as computer technology advances.": Text 2 presents distant reading as a tool to expand inquiry, not as evidence that current methods are obsolete. Text 1 says nothing about methodology becoming outdated. ✗
  • "It must widen its focus to include aspects of modernist fiction beyond style, a productive but overrepresented site of inquiry.": both authors focus specifically on stylistic representation. Neither calls for moving beyond style — they want broader attention within style. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
